articles.title: India setting new benchmarks in medical research: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh
articles.sum: India's health system has emerged as more self-reliant, accessible, affordable, modern and people-centric as compared to earlier times says Defence Minister Rajnath Singh

articles.title: Kuki-Zo Council meets IB chief, seeks govt. action on blockade by Nagas
articles.sum: Kuki-Zo Council members say restoration of the popular government in Manipur on February 4, a year after President’s Rule was imposed, has been of “no good” to the people

articles.title: We will face the 2028 elections under the leadership of former CM Siddaramaiah, says Minister
articles.sum: No question of ignoring former CM, says Satish Jarkiholi

articles.title: Social media platforms must follow SOPs on child abuse content: Government
articles.sum: Social media platforms must follow child abuse material reporting rules. Timely reporting and coordination with law enforcement are key duties. The government is examining Meta's response to Instagram ad concerns. Officials focus on platform compliance with material detection obl...

articles.title: Ram Mandir Trust invites CEO applications: Must be a 'practising Hindu, Ram bhakt'
articles.sum: The Ram Janmbhoomi Teerth Kshetra trust is seeking applications for its first chief executive officer. This professional role aims to enhance the management of the Ayodhya Ram temple operations. The trust decided to appoint a CEO after recent allegations of donation theft by cash...

articles.title: AAP launches signature drive to seek death for Ayodhya donation thieves
articles.sum: Arvind Kejriwal has initiated a nationwide signature campaign demanding capital punishment for those accused of theft. He launched the drive in Delhi, signing an open letter to Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Kejriwal urged citizens to organize Hanuman Chalisa recitations and gathe...

articles.title: One Indian crew member killed, 6 injured after Iran strikes UAE tankers in Strait of Hormuz
articles.sum: Two UAE-flagged tankers were struck by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters. One Indian crew member died and eight others sustained injuries in the attack. The UAE condemned the incident as a serious violation of international law. This escalation occurs amid rising military t...

articles.title: Citing former PM Manmohan Singh, BJP says Congress has habit of discrediting Election Commission
articles.sum: The BJP cited Manmohan Singh's remarks to accuse Congress of attacking institutions. Congress countered by referencing Quraishi's book to criticize Prime Minister Modi. The BJP alleged ministers targeted the poll body when the UPA was in power. Salman Khurshid was censured for pr...

articles.title: Anti-graft bills not aimed at undercutting non-BJP governments, says ministry of home affairs
articles.sum: The home ministry has rejected opposition claims regarding anti-corruption bills. These proposed laws aim to remove arrested officials after thirty days in custody. Legislative membership remains intact, ensuring government stability is unaffected. The ministry asserts the mandat...

articles.title: Is women's Test cricket struggling for relevance?
articles.sum: The scheduling of the game and the announcement of Brendon McCullum's sacking left the historic Lord's Test between England and India struggling for relevance, so what is the future of women's Test cricket?
